rule: "Operator's history" refers to the history of the specific legal entity (operator) and does not transfer between entities upon the sale of mine assets.

An operator's history of previous violations is based on both the total number of violations (where each distinct infraction counts as one violation) and the number of repeat violations of the same citable provision (the specific subsection of a standard) that occurred within the 15 months immediately preceding the date of the violation being assessed.

Only assessed violations that have been paid in full (with funds cleared), finally adjudicated (as to both liability and penalty), or have become final orders of the Commission (and remain so at the time of history calculation) will be included in determining an operator's history.
